answer: A grasper retracts the gallbladder fundus superiorly against the liver while another grasper holds the infundibulum laterally to set tension. A hook/white-tipped dissector opens fibrofatty peritoneal tissue around the cystic duct region and along the gallbladder–liver interface, widening the dissection plane with intermittent minor bleeding. bipolar coagulates visible vessels and bleeding points, and the irrigator clears bloody fluid. Continued traction exposes the cystic duct region, a gray tubular structure, and inflamed liver-bed attachments for further separation.
